name: Artipie
cost: Free (MIT open-source, self-hosted). A free hosted instance at central.artipie.com lets you sign in to host private repos; no published paid pricing tiers.
last_updated: 2025-02-25 (last commit on master); latest release v1.17.16 on 2024-04-26; GitHub repo metadata shows a later push on 2025-09-17 (non-master branch/tags). Repo largely dormant since early 2025.
perf_notes: Reactive, non-blocking architecture on Vert.x; streams artifacts rather than buffering whole files in memory. Micrometer-based HTTP/storage/JVM metrics are exposed in Prometheus format. No official published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks found.
scale_notes: Self-described as "horizontally scalable"; intended to run stateless with shared object storage (S3) so multiple instances can serve the same data. No built-in cluster coordination, leader election, or distributed locking documented; HA relies on the shared storage backend (S3/etcd). etcd backend is limited to blobs under ~10MB and intended for configuration.
summary: An MIT-licensed, reactive-Java multi-ecosystem binary repository manager (PyPI, Maven, Docker, npm, NuGet, Go, Helm, RubyGems, Composer, RPM, Debian, conda, ...) that runs as a single fat jar or Docker image with no database, storing artifacts on filesystem or S3 and serving a PyPI simple index for pip/twine.
extras: Multi-ecosystem in one server: Maven, Docker, npm, NuGet, Go, Helm, RubyGems, Composer, RPM, Debian/apt, Anaconda/conda, PyPI
pypi-proxy repository type: caching pull-through proxy of upstream PyPI that stores downloaded packages and keeps them available when the remote is down
REST management API with Swagger UI (port 8086) for repos/users/roles/storage-aliases
Pluggable credentials: YAML file users (plain or sha256 passwords), GitHub personal-access-token auth, environment-variable user, and Keycloak (OIDC Direct Access Grants password flow)
RBAC policy with users + roles, wildcard permissions, read/write/delete action synonyms, plus Docker- and REST-API-specific permission classes
Storage aliases (_storages.yaml) so repo maintainers reference a storage by name without seeing credentials
etcd backend usable for configuration/small-blob storage
GPG signing of repository metadata for Debian/RPM adapters
Legacy PyPI XML-RPC search endpoint (pip-search style) implemented in the pypi-adapter (SearchSlice), returning text/xml methodResponse results
Kubernetes-friendly health endpoints (/-/health, /-/readiness, /-/liveness) checking config-storage availability
Distributed as both a standard and Ubuntu-based Docker image and as a fat (uber) jar
